terms.definition: React SDK providing prebuilt UI primitives (Player, Broadcast) for video experiences in web apps.
Top 100 orchestrators by total bonded stake eligible to receive work each protocol round.
Strategic bodies aligning ecosystem stakeholders on roadmap and opportunities through structured strategy-setting.
Software component executing AI model inference tasks on an orchestrator node.
Portion of the Livepeer network dedicated to AI inference work, where orchestrators advertise AI capabilities and gateways route AI jobs.
Special Purpose Entity funded by the community treasury to advance decentralized AI compute, scaling ComfyStream and BYOC pipelines.
Community outreach initiative where participants represent Livepeer to new audiences.
A Layer 2 Optimistic Rollup settling to Ethereum, processing transactions off-chain with Ethereum-grade security.
Community-curated list of projects, tutorials, demos, and resources in the Livepeer ecosystem.
AI system independently pursuing complex goals by perceiving, deciding, using tools, and acting without supervision.
Staking (locking) LPT tokens to an orchestrator in Livepeer's delegated proof-of-stake system.
Reward for completing a specific task, funded by community treasury or foundation.
Bring-Your-Own-Container — deployment pattern where users supply custom Docker containers for AI workloads on the Livepeer network.
Percentage of rewards or fees an orchestrator retains before distributing the remainder to delegators.
ComfyUI custom node running real-time media workflows for AI-powered video and audio on live streams.
Free, load-balanced, geo-distributed RPC service for Arbitrum L2 and Ethereum L1, maintained by the LiveInfra SPE.
Specification for structured commit messages enabling automated changelogs.
Organization governed by smart contracts, with rules encoded in code rather than legal structures.
Livepeer's hosted real-time AI video platform turning live camera input into AI-transformed visuals with sub-second latency.
Token holder who stakes LPT to an orchestrator to secure the network, participate in governance, and earn rewards.
LPT holders staking tokens toward orchestrators they trust, sharing in rewards without running infrastructure.
LPT holders assigning their staked tokens to a chosen orchestrator, sharing rewards without operating infrastructure.
Recurring meeting where core developers discuss protocol development, node releases, and roadmap.
Blockchain analytics platform for SQL queries on on-chain data.
Automatic switching to a backup node or system when the primary fails, maintaining service continuity.
Percentage of ETH fees an orchestrator retains (fee cut) versus the share distributed to delegators (fee share).
Node that submits jobs, routes work to orchestrators, manages payment flows, and provides a protocol interface for applications.
Person or organisation running and maintaining gateway nodes for network access.
System of rules and processes for protocol decision-making, including on-chain voting via LivepeerGovernor.
An AI platform and open-source community providing model repositories, datasets, and inference APIs; a primary source for AI models deployed on Livepeer orchestrator nodes.
The Livepeer Forum's governance category for LIPs, pre-proposals, and protocol governance discussions.
Meta-governance SPE ensuring transparent management of Livepeer governance and treasury allocation.
Open-source visualization and dashboarding platform for metrics and logs.
Non-repayable allocation from the community treasury or Livepeer Foundation for ecosystem development contributions.
Specialized processor for parallel computation used in video transcoding and AI inference workloads.
Gateway Wizard SPE building a managed DevOps tool for running and managing gateway infrastructure.
Per-round rate of new LPT issuance, adjusting dynamically around the 50% target bonding rate.
Newly minted LPT distributed each protocol round proportional to stake.
A high-level interpreted scripting language used for web and server-side development; Livepeer's primary SDKs and gateway clients expose JavaScript/TypeScript APIs.
Know Your Orchestrator — Live Pioneers interview series profiling active orchestrators on the network.
Livepeer Improvement Proposal — formal design document for protocol changes.
LIP introducing the on-chain LivepeerGovernor contract and community treasury mechanism.
SPE providing ongoing ecosystem infrastructure contributions with monthly progress updates.
Independent community for long-term LPT holders producing educational content and guides.
SPE providing free, reliable blockchain infrastructure services including the Community Arbitrum Node.
JavaScript/TypeScript library for the Livepeer AI API enabling AI inference integration.
Python library for the Livepeer AI API providing programmatic access to AI inference pipelines.
Go server-side SDK for the Livepeer Studio API.
JavaScript library for the Livepeer Studio API providing programmatic access to stream and asset management.
Official protocol explorer for viewing orchestrator state, staking data, and governance proposals.
Non-profit Cayman Islands Foundation Company stewarding long-term vision, ecosystem growth, and core development.
Original company that built Livepeer's initial architecture and protocol.
ERC-20 governance and staking token for orchestrator selection, delegation, reward distribution, and network security.
New LPT tokens minted each round via inflation, distributed to active orchestrators and their delegators.
Livepeer's algorithm for decentralized token distribution at genesis using Merkle proofs.
Funding released incrementally upon achievement of predefined deliverables rather than in a lump sum.
Open-source media server providing RTMP ingest, HLS output, and multi-protocol streaming support.
Protocol-governed pool of LPT funded by inflation for community-approved ecosystem development.
Software distributed with its source code under a licence permitting study, modification, and redistribution; Livepeer's protocol, go-livepeer node software, and SDK libraries are all open source.
Supply-side operator contributing GPU resources, receiving jobs, performing transcoding or AI inference, and earning rewards.
Minimum "for" vote percentage (excluding abstentions) required for a governance proposal to pass.
Informal governance discussion document posted on the Forum before a formal LIP or treasury proposal.
Fundamental pricing unit for transcoding: cost in wei for processing one pixel of video.
Lottery-like payment model where only winning tickets are redeemed on-chain, amortizing per-segment costs across many transactions.
Open-source monitoring system collecting time-series metrics via HTTP pull from instrumented targets.
Model where participants prove they performed useful work for the network rather than relying solely on stake size.
Mechanism where matching funds amplify small individual contributions, prioritizing broadly supported projects over whale-funded ones.
Minimum stake participation threshold required for a governance vote to be considered valid.
Running AI models on live streaming input with latency low enough for interactive speeds, typically under 100ms.
Funding model rewarding projects after they demonstrate value, reducing speculative risk for the treasury.
On-chain transaction an active orchestrator submits each round to mint and distribute new LPT to themselves and their delegators.
Percentage of inflationary LPT rewards an orchestrator retains before distributing the remainder to delegators.
Formal solicitation posted by the community or Foundation inviting teams to submit proposals for defined work.
Discrete time interval (measured in Ethereum/Arbitrum blocks) for calculating staking rewards and protocol state transitions.
Protocol allowing a program to execute a procedure on a remote server; in blockchain contexts, the mechanism for querying and submitting transactions to a node.
Penalty mechanism destroying a portion of an orchestrator's bonded LPT for protocol violations.
Treasury-funded organizational unit with a defined scope, budget, and accountability structure operating within Livepeer governance.
Governance services firm serving as first GovWorks Chair, building transparent governance frameworks for Livepeer.
Voting system where each vote is proportional to the voter's bonded LPT stake.
Locking tokens in a proof-of-stake protocol to participate in network security, governance, and earn rewards.
Project building the video layer for decentralized social platforms, focused on the AT Protocol ecosystem.
Concentrated treasury spending approach targeting high-impact growth initiatives during strategic market windows.
Liquid staking protocol for LPT allowing staking while maintaining liquidity through derivative tokens.
Smart contract enforcing a mandatory delay between governance proposal passage and on-chain execution.
Economic design of a token system encompassing supply, distribution, incentives, staking, inflation, and governance.
Direct digital-to-digital conversion of video from one encoding to another, producing multiple adaptive renditions.
SPE seeding new contribution mechanisms, coordinating talent, and directing budgets for ecosystem workstreams.
Pool of LPT and ETH held on-chain for funding public goods and ecosystem development, governed by token holder votes.
Forum section for treasury proposals, SPE funding discussions, and resource allocation.
Governable percentage of per-round LPT inflation diverted to the community treasury (currently 10%).
Recurring community call focused on treasury discussions and SPE updates.
Waiting period during which tokens are locked after initiating unbonding before becoming withdrawable (7 rounds in Livepeer).
The official currency of the United States; used as the reference denomination for Livepeer gateway fees, grant amounts, treasury allocations, and market data.
Proposed mechanism locking LPT for enhanced voting power, aligning long-term incentive structures.
Delegators overriding their orchestrator's governance vote with their own independent stake-weighted vote.
Number of rounds (1) between governance proposal creation and the start of the voting period.
Number of rounds (10) during which token holders can cast votes on a governance proposal.
Weight of a participant's governance vote, determined by their bonded LPT stake at the time of the vote.
Biweekly informal community call for development updates and ecosystem news.
Neural network representing and predicting environment dynamics, enabling an agent to plan by simulating outcomes.
Focused execution teams organized around specific domains, translating Advisory Board recommendations into phased initiatives.
